data.title: Inotiv signals continued DSA revenue growth and margin improvements amid RMS site consolidations and robust awards momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Inotiv, Inc. (NOTV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Leasure reported a strong year-over-year increase in demand for the Discovery & Safety Assessment (DSA) business, highlighting "a strong year-over-year increase in demand for our Discovery & Safety Assessment business" and sustained execution on goals from the May 2025 Investor Day, with a focus on "improving our cash flow and margins and maintaining our focus on customer metrics."
  • Leasure emphasized the ongoing RMS site consolidation, noting, "Phase 2 of our RMS site consolidation project has remained on track. In early October, we closed one of the three planned RMS facilities and now have two additional lease facilities remaining to close."
  • Addressing business disruptions, Leasure stated, "On August 18, we filed an 8-K disclosing that we became aware of a cybersecurity incident which caused disruption to certain of our business operations. We worked to restore availability and access to our networks and systems during this fiscal fourth quarter."
  • Leasure also announced engagement with Perella Weinberg Partners for debt refinancing alternatives and referenced settlements in pending litigation, explaining that settlement payments "will be fully covered by insurance."
  • CFO Beth Taylor provided key financial results: "For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, total revenue was $138.1 million compared to $130.4 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024. This was a $7.7 million or 5.9% increase in revenue from the prior year quarter, primarily driven by increased revenue of $7.1 million within our DSA segment."

Outlook

  • Management is not providing formal fiscal 2026 guidance at this time, with Taylor stating, "As we have stated previously, we hope to resume providing guidance once we have greater clarity on the market and client demand and clarity on any impact to our business once there is more information on tariffs."
  • Leasure noted ongoing positive trends into the first two months of the current quarter, saying, "we are coming off some very weak numbers from a year ago and the geopolitical and macroeconomic conditions, risk and uncertainties are likely to remain with the industry for the foreseeable future."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q4 2025 reached $138.1 million, up $7.7 million or 5.9% year-over-year, with DSA revenue at $51.6 million, a 15.7% increase from the prior year period; RMS revenue was $86.5 million, up 0.8%.
  • DSA segment net new awards for Q4 were $54.2 million, a 61% year-over-year increase; the DSA backlog conversion rate reached 37.4% for the quarter—the highest in three years.
  • Operating loss for Q4 2025 was $6.8 million, narrowing from $13.2 million in Q4 2024; consolidated net loss attributable to common shareholders was $8.6 million or ($0.25) per diluted share, compared to ($0.73) per share last year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025 was $11.8 million or 8.5% of revenue, more than doubling the prior year’s Q4 figure.
  • Cash from operations in Q4 was $14.3 million, raising the cash balance to $21.7 million by quarter end.

Q&A

  • Frank Takkinen, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: Asked about headwinds and the impact of cybersecurity expenses. Leasure responded, "the major headwind was for us in the early August finding out the cybersecurity incident we reported...it's really those intangible costs and the time it takes for organization to focus on that...it would be naive to think that it did have some impact on our earnings, our expenses and some of our awards."
  • Takkinen followed up on quarter-to-date trends. Leasure clarified, "our quarter ended December 30 is typically our weakest quarter during the holidays...we have an increase in awards of over 37% over the last 9 months."
  • Matthew Hewitt, Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC, Research Division: Queried exposure to monoclonal antibodies in light of FDA guidance. Leasure stated, "our revenue related to monoclonal antibodies is minimal, very small if any."
  • Hewitt asked about customer budgets for the coming year. Leasure reported, "I do see an increasing amount of reoccurring customers, which is also very encouraging."
  • David Windley, Jefferies LLC, Research Division: Sought details on DSA lead times and RMS margin impact. Leasure explained, "large animal safety assessment capacity is operating at a very high level of capacity at the moment," and RMS margin fluctuations relate to "cost of the NHPs that we're bringing in" and associated operating expenses.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions focused on headwinds, expense management, and sector uncertainties, with a neutral to slightly positive tone and some pressing for clarity on business disruptions and margin trends.
  • Management’s tone was generally confident and constructive, acknowledging challenges while emphasizing operational improvements and resilience. Leasure repeatedly expressed satisfaction with recent momentum, stating, "I was very pleased how quickly we recovered."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed more confidence regarding DSA growth and margin improvements, while analyst sentiment remained cautiously optimistic.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reported increased DSA revenue and awards momentum, with DSA revenue up 15.7% versus a prior 8.9% quarterly year-over-year gain, and awards up 61% from 25% in Q3.
  • RMS revenue growth slowed to 0.8% from 34.1% in Q3, but margins benefited from continued site consolidation and cost actions.
  • Operating loss narrowed significantly from the previous quarter, and adjusted EBITDA improved.
  • Management tone shifted from "cautiously optimistic" to more assertive confidence, citing improved metrics and ongoing operational gains.
  • Analysts continued to focus on sustainability of recent growth, execution risks, and sector headwinds, maintaining a similar level of skepticism as before.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing geopolitical and macroeconomic risks, as well as uncertainties around tariffs and sector demand.
  • The cybersecurity incident was highlighted as a significant headwind, with Leasure noting intangible and not fully quantifiable impacts on operations and expenses.
  • RMS margins remain subject to variability in NHP costs and external factors such as tariffs, transportation, and quarantine requirements.

Final Takeaway

The company highlighted strong DSA revenue and awards growth, sequential improvement in margins, and continued operational execution despite the temporary headwinds from a cybersecurity incident and challenging market conditions. Management remains focused on RMS site consolidations, technology streamlining, and debt refinancing, with a commitment to further improving margins and client experiences moving forward.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: MDU Resources prices public offering of 10.15M shares
data.description:
• MDU Resources (MDU) stated on Thursday that it has priced an underwritten public offering of 10.15M shares of its common stock at a price of $19.70 per share and an additional purchase option of 1.52M shares to its underwriters.
  • Wells Fargo Securities, BofA Securities, and J.P. Morgan are acting as joint lead bookrunners of the offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 5, 2025.
  • The shares are expected to be borrowed by the forward sellers, Wells Fargo Securities, BofA Securities, and J.P. Morgan from third parties and sold to the underwriters in the offering in connection with the forward sale agreements.
  • Settlement of the forward sale agreements is expected to occur no later than 24 months following the completion of the offering.
  • If the company elects physical settlement of the forward sale agreements, it expects to use any net proceeds for general corporate purposes like repayment of debt, capital expenditures, and acquisitions, including payment in 2026 for a 49% undivided ownership interest in the Badger Wind Farm project, as well as working capital.

data.title: Vodacom agrees to $2.1B Safaricom stake purchase from Kenyan government and Vodafone
data.description:
• Vodacom (OTCPK:VODAF) has entered into an agreement with the Government of Kenya to acquire 15% of Safaricom PLC and an additional 5% from Vodafone (VOD) at KES34 per share, valuing the deal at $2.1 billion (R36 billion).
  • Should the transaction receive requisite approvals from regulatory and governmental authorities in Kenya, Ethiopia, and South Africa, this will see Vodacom’s (OTCPK:VODAF) stake in Safaricom, which will remain listed on the Nairobi Stock Exchange, increase from 35% to 55%, the company said in a statement.
  • The move is a key milestone in Vodacom’s Vision2030 strategy, which includes deepening its leadership in Africa’s high-growth markets and scaling its diversified portfolio.
  • Safaricom’s financial results will transition from being accounted for on an associate basis to being fully consolidated, increasing Vodacom Group’s revenue towards R220 billion.

data.title: Salesforce in charts: Subscription and support revenue rises 9.5% Y/Y
Snowflake strikes $200 million AI deal with Anthropic
data.description:
• Snowflake (SNOW) and Anthropic (ANTHRO) announced a multi-year, $200 million partnership to expand integration of Anthropic's Claude AI models into Snowflake's Cortex AI platform, enabling agentic AI for global enterprises.
  • This deal builds on prior collaborations, where over 12,600 customers already process trillions of Claude tokens monthly, now focusing on multi-step reasoning agents for complex data analysis on sensitive enterprise data. 
  •  “Anthropic joins a very select group of partners where we have nine-figure alignment, co-innovation at the product level, and a proven track record of executing together for customers worldwide. Together, the combined power of Snowflake and Claude is raising the bar for how enterprises deploy scalable, context-aware AI on top of their most critical business data.” said Sridhar Ramaswamy, CEO, Snowflake.

data.title: Coniagas announces $1M life offering
data.description:
• Coniagas Battery Metals (COS:CA) announced on Thursday a private placement of  17.19M units of the company at a price of $0.06 per unit for gross proceeds of up to $1.03M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder to acquire an additional common share at a price of $0.08/share for a period of 5 years from the date of issuance.
  • The gross proceeds of the offering will be used to fund the advancement of its Graal property and for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The offering is scheduled to close on or about December 19, 2025.
  • In connection with the offering, the company has entered into a finder's fee agreement with Research Capital and agreed to pay the finder a cash commission equal to 8.0% of the gross proceeds and to issue to the finder such number of non-transferable common share purchase warrants.
  • Finder's warrant entitles the holder thereof to purchase one common share at a price of $0.06/share for a period of 5 years following the date of issuance.
  • The company also announced that it has entered into an agreement with the Finder dated November 27, 2025, pursuant to which the Finder has agreed to provide financial advisory services.
  • The company has agreed to pay the Finder a cash fee of $25,000 and issue the Finder an  0.33M common shares.

data.title: Orla Mining declares inaugural quarterly cash dividend of US$0.015 per share
data.description:
• Orla Mining (ORLA) declares $0.015/share inaugural quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 0.45%
  • Payable Feb. 10; to shareholders of record Jan. 12; ex-div Jan. 12.
  • The board of directors has also approved a policy under which the company intends to pay a regular quarterly dividend of US$0.015 per share, or US$0.06 per share annually.
  • "This initial return of capital represents a significant milestone in Orla's growth. Our rapid evolution from exploration to a diversified gold producer reflects both the strength of our strategy and the discipline of our execution. As construction of the South Railroad Project moves forward in 2026/2027, we will be operating in three countries across North America with a continued focus on generating sustainable value for our shareholders," said Jason Simpson, President & CEO.
  • See ORLA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Australia's October trade surplus widens to AUD 4.39B; robust exports outpace record imports
data.description:
Australia's goods trade surplus widened to AUD 4.39 billion in October 2025, surpassing market expectations (AUD 4.2 billion) and the revised September figure (AUD 3.71 billion). This was the largest surplus since July, driven by exports rising faster than imports. 

Exports grew 3.4% month-on-month to a two-year high of AUD 45.98 billion in October 2025, after a downwardly revised 7.6% rise in September, driven by a 14.2% increase in non-monetary gold shipments to AUD 6.10 billion, following a 62.2% surge in September, amid rising prices. 

Australia’s goods imports rose 2.0% month-on-month to a fresh high of AUD 41.59 billion in October 2025, quickening from an upwardly revised 1.8% growth in the previous month amid robust domestic demand and early festive-season stocking. 

Among trading partners, exports increased to South Korea (39.1%), India (70.6%), and Japan (10.9%), while exports to the United States fell 18.5% due to the impact of newly imposed tariffs. Shipments to China, Australia’s top trading partner, also dropped by 2.2%.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.27% to around 8,570 on Thursday, halting its two-session winning streak, as losses in gold stocks and rate-sensitive sectors offset strength in miners. The Australian dollar strengthened toward $0.661 on Thursday, extending its rally to its highest level in two months.

data.title: Sam Altman tried buying a rival to Elon Musk's SpaceX - report
data.description:
Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI (OPENAI), explored a surprise move into space sector by gathering funds to either acquire or partner with a rocket company called Stoke Space, according to a Wall Street Journal report that cites people familiar with the matter. However, after advanced talks, the deal was called off earlier this year.

Stoke Space—a rocket startup founded by former Blue Origin employees—focuses on fully reusable rockets and positions itself as an emerging competitor to Elon Musk's SpaceX (SPACE).

Sam Altman aimed to gain a controlling stake through potentially billions in equity investments; however, the discussions ended without a deal, the report added.

Altman has long been interested in building orbital data centers to power AI while minimizing environmental impact, tying into his interest in space technology beyond just launch services. The tech enthusiast is supporting another space startup, Longshot Space, that develops innovative satellite deployment technology that does not rely on traditional rocket combustion methods.

The rivalry between Sam Altman and Elon Musk has been on edge for a long time now with shared history as OpenAI (OPENAI) co-founders and competition between xAI and OpenAI. 

The proposed partnership with Stoke would place Altman in more direct competition with Musk. Besides, Merge Labs—a brain-computer interface business—rivals Elon's Neuralink, and OpenAI (OPENAI) is also building a social network that could compete with X.

data.title: Asia stocks subdued despite Wall Street gains, Japanese shares surge on tech and chipmaker gains amid rate hikes talk
data.description:
Asia stock market were largely subdued on Thursday, taking cues from a positive lead on Wall Street amid expectations that the US Federal Reserve will deliver a December rate cut, while Japanese shares rose sharply with gains in tech, chipmakers.

Traders also monitored the yen, which continued to edge higher amid speculation that the Bank of Japan could raise rates later this month.

Investors are also assessing the prospect of White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett replacing Fed Chair Jerome Powell in May, a move that could signal a push for more aggressive easing. 

Gold edged above $4,210 per ounce on Thursday, staying near a six-week high as investors grew more confident of a Federal Reserve rate cut next week. 

The benchmark KOSPI slipped 0.80% to around 4,003 on Thursday, erasing gains from earlier in the week as weakness in chip and automotive stocks weighed on the index. 

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 1.93% to above 50,100 on Thursday, extending gains from the previous session. The Japanese yen strengthened toward 155 per dollar on Thursday, trading near its highest level in more than two weeks, amid growing speculation that the Bank of Japan will raise interest rates this month.

Expectations firmed after BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda voiced confidence in Japan’s economic outlook and said the central bank would carefully weigh the pros and cons of a rate hike and act as appropriate.

Technology stocks led the advance, with notable gains from SoftBank Group (2.9%), Lasertec (1.7%), Kioxia Holdings (1.2%), Disco Corp (3.1%) and Tokyo Electron (1.7%). Robot makers also posted strong gains on renewed market interest, including Fanuc (8.8%), Yaskawa Electric (8.5%) and Nabtesco (9.3%).

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.07% to around 3860, while the Shenzhen Component rose 0.1% to 12,970 on Thursday, and the offshore yuan weakened to around 7.05 per dollar, retreating from a more than one-year high in the prior session after the People’s Bank of China set a weaker-than-expected daily fixing.

Meanwhile, investors are keenly anticipating official signals on Beijing’s economic policy and setting of growth targets for the upcoming year, with attention focused on two pivotal events: the annual Central Economic Work Conference and the December Politburo meeting.

Economists widely expect China to maintain a consistent approach to its headline economic goals. The consensus forecast suggests that Beijing will reaffirm an "around 5%" annual growth target for 2026.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.15% to 25,834 in Thursday morning deals, reversing a sharp drop the previous day as gains mainly from tech and consumer stocks lifted risk appetite.

Locally, economists expect Hong Kong’s HIBOR to stay between 2-3% in the coming quarters, providing a stable backdrop for asset markets and the broader economy. 

India (SENSEX) rose 0.41% to 85,256 in volatile Thursday morning trade, halting a four-session losing streak ahead of the RBI monetary policy decision on Friday.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.27% to around 8,570 on Thursday, halting its two-session winning streak, as losses in gold stocks and rate-sensitive sectors offset strength in miners. The Australian dollar strengthened toward $0.661 on Thursday, extending its rally to its highest level in two months.

Australia's goods trade surplus widened to AUD 4.39 billion in October 2025, surpassing market expectations (AUD 4.2 billion) and the revised September figure (AUD 3.71 billion). This was the largest surplus since July, driven by exports rising faster than imports. Total exports grew 3.4% month-over-month to a two-year high of AUD 45.98 billion in October.

Gold miners Newmont and Northern Star both fell approximately 1.9%, dragging the broader gold sector down 1.3%. The financials and real estate sectors also retreated, dropping 0.4% and 2.2%, respectively, with three of the "Big Four" banks closing lower and property giant Goodman Group slipping 1.5%.

Household spending in Australia grew 1.3% mom in October 2025, picking up from an upwardly revised 0.3% gain in the prior month.

The central bank is set to meet next week, with rates already considered remaining at 3.6% after three cuts this year. 

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes extended their recovery after softer-than-expected ADP data showed a surprise 32,000 drop in private payrolls, pushing Fed cut odds close to a certainty. The Dow rose 0.9%, the S&P 500 gained 0.4%, and the Nasdaq advanced 0.2%. Financial and defensive sectors outperformed as investors reweighted near-term policy risk.

U.S. stock futures were steady on Thursday after another winning session on Wall Street: Dow +0.11%; S&P 500 +0.06%; Nasdaq -0.05%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).
